message.content: repository 外だったら ghq をパイプさせたら便利なんじゃない？

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

aiTitle: Use ghq with pipe outside repository

content.content:
1	# fzf helpers for Entire CLI sessions.
2	#
3	# Usage:
4	#   etf    # session picker -> action picker (resume/explain/checkpoints/info/stop/clean)
5	#   etfd   # entire dispatch --local
6	#   etfr   # pick branch, resume session, then reopen the matching agent when supported
7	
8	_entire_session_list() {
9	  command entire session list --json 2>/dev/null |
10	    jq -r '
11	      def ts_parts($ts):
12	        ($ts | tostring | try capture("^(?<y>[0-9]{4})-(?<m>[0-9]{2})-(?<d>[0-9]{2})[T ](?<h>[0-9]{2}):(?<min>[0-9]{2})") catch null);
13	      def fmt($ts):
14	        (ts_parts($ts) as $p | if $p == null then "" else "\($p.m)-\($p.d) \($p.h):\($p.min)" end);
15	      def year($ts):
16	        (ts_parts($ts) as $p | if $p == null then "" else $p.y end);
17	      .[] |
18	      [
19	        (.session_id // ""),
20	        (.status // ""),
21	        (.agent // ""),
22	        (fmt(.started_at // .last_active // "")),
23	        (fmt(.ended_at // "") // "-" | if . == "" then "-" else . end),
24	        (year(.started_at // .last_active // ""))
25	      ] | @tsv
26	    '
27	}
28	
29	_entire_session_table() {
30	  _entire_session_list |
31	    awk -F '\t' '
32	      BEGIN {
33	        printf "__HEADER__\t%10s\t%14s\t%14s\t%14s\t%6s\n", "status", "agent", "started_at", "ended_at", "year"
34	      }
35	      {
36	        printf "%s\t%10s\t%14s\t%14s\t%14s\t%6s\n", $1, $2, $3, $4, $5, $6
37	      }
38	    '
39	}
40	
41	_entire_session_fzf() {
42	  local preview_window="$1"
43	
44	  _entire_session_table |
45	    fzf --ansi \
46	      --header-lines=1 \
47	      --with-nth=2.. \
48	      --delimiter=$'\t' \
49	      --prompt='entire session> ' \
50	      --footer='Enter: select session | Ctrl-/: toggle preview' \
51	      --bind='ctrl-/:toggle-preview' \
52	      --preview='entire session info {1} --json 2>/dev/null | jq -C . || true' \
53	      --preview-window="$preview_window"
54	}
55	
56	_entire_action_pick() {
57	  printf '%s\n' \
58	    'resume       : resume session and reopen matching agent' \
59	    'explain      : explain latest checkpoint' \
60	    'checkpoints  : pick checkpoint to explain' \
61	    'info         : session info' \
62	    'stop         : stop session' \
63	    'clean        : entire clean --session' |
64	    fzf --ansi \
65	      --prompt='entire action> ' \
66	      --delimiter=':' \
67	      --with-nth=1,2 \
68	      --height=50% \
69	      --reverse \
70	      --footer='Enter: run selected action'
71	}
72	
73	_entire_branch_pick() {
74	  local current_branch selected
75	  current_branch=$(git branch --show-current 2>/dev/null)
76	
77	  selected=$(
78	    git for-each-ref --format='%(refname)' --sort=-committerdate refs/heads refs/remotes/origin 2>/dev/null |
79	      sed -e '/^refs\/remotes\/origin\/HEAD$/d' -e 's#^refs/heads/##' -e 's#^refs/remotes/origin/##' |
80	      awk -v cur="$current_branch" 'NF && $0 !~ /^entire\// && !seen[$0]++ { printf "%s\t%s\n", ($0 == cur ? "*" : " "), $0 }' |
81	      fzf --ansi \
82	        --delimiter=$'\t' \
83	        --with-nth=1,2 \
84	        --prompt='entire branch> ' \
85	        --height=40% \
86	        --reverse \
87	        --preview='git -c color.ui=always log --color=always --oneline --decorate -n 20 {2} 2>/dev/null || git -c color.ui=always log --color=always --oneline --decorate -n 20 origin/{2} 2>/dev/null || echo "No commit preview available"' \
88	        --preview-window='down:60%:wrap' \
89	        --footer='Enter: run entire session resume on selected branch'
90	  ) || return
91	
92	  [[ -n "$selected" ]] || return
93	  cut -f2 <<< "$selected"
94	}
95	
96	_entire_checkpoint_list_by_session() {
97	  local session_id
98	  session_id="$1"
99	
100	  [[ -n "$session_id" ]] || return 1
101	  command entire checkpoint list --session "$session_id"
102	}
103	
104	_entire_checkpoint_table_by_session() {
105	  _entire_checkpoint_list_by_session "$1" |
106	    awk '
107	      BEGIN {
108	        reset = "\033[0m"
109	        checkpoint_id_color = ENVIRON["ENTIRE_ZSH_CHECKPOINT_ID_COLOR"]
110	        if (ENVIRON["NO_COLOR"] != "") {
111	          checkpoint_id_color = ""
112	          reset = ""
113	        } else if (checkpoint_id_color == "") {
114	          checkpoint_id_color = "\033[33m"
115	        }
116	        printf "%s\t%s\t%s\n", "checkpoint_id", "checkpoint_id", "message"
117	      }
118	      /^●[[:space:]]+/ {
119	        checkpoint_id = $2
120	        message = $0
121	        sub(/^●[[:space:]]+[^[:space:]]+[[:space:]]+/, "", message)
122	        printf "%s\t%s\t%s\n", checkpoint_id, checkpoint_id_color checkpoint_id reset, message
123	      }
124	    '
125	}
126	
127	_entire_checkpoint_id_from_line() {
128	  awk '{ if ($1 == "●") print $2; else print $1; exit }'
129	}
130	
131	_entire_latest_checkpoint_id_by_session() {
132	  local session_id checkpoint_id
133	  session_id="$1"
134	
135	  [[ -n "$session_id" ]] || return 1
136	
137	  checkpoint_id=$(
138	    _entire_checkpoint_list_by_session "$session_id" |
139	      awk '/^●[[:space:]]+/ { print; exit }' |
140	      _entire_checkpoint_id_from_line
141	  )
142	
143	  [[ -n "$checkpoint_id" ]] || {
144	    print -u2 'No checkpoint found for selected session.'
145	    return 1
146	  }
147	
148	  print -r -- "$checkpoint_id"
149	}
150	
151	_entire_checkpoint_pick_by_session() {
152	  local session_id line checkpoint_id
153	  session_id="$1"
154	
155	  [[ -n "$session_id" ]] || return 1
156	
157	  line=$(
158	    _entire_checkpoint_table_by_session "$session_id" |
159	      fzf --ansi \
160	        --header-lines=1 \
161	        --with-nth=2,3 \
162	        --delimiter=$'\t' \
163	        --prompt='entire checkpoint> ' \
164	        --height=50% \
165	        --reverse \
166	        --preview='entire checkpoint explain {1} 2>/dev/null || true' \
167	        --preview-window='down:70%:wrap' \
168	        --footer='Enter: explain selected checkpoint'
169	  ) || return
170	
171	  [[ -n "$line" ]] || return
172	  checkpoint_id=$(print -r -- "$line" | _entire_checkpoint_id_from_line)
173	  [[ -n "$checkpoint_id" ]] || return 1
174	
175	  print -r -- "$checkpoint_id"
176	}
177	
178	_entire_branch_by_session() {
179	  local session_id worktree_path branch
180	  session_id="$1"
181	
182	  [[ -n "$session_id" ]] || return 1
183	
184	  worktree_path=$(command entire session info "$session_id" --json 2>/dev/null | jq -r '.worktree_path // empty')
185	
186	  [[ -n "$worktree_path" ]] || {
187	    print -u2 "Could not find worktree_path for session: $session_id"
188	    return 1
189	  }
190	
191	  branch=$(git -C "$worktree_path" branch --show-current 2>/dev/null)
192	
193	  [[ -n "$branch" ]] || {
194	    print -u2 "Could not determine branch for session: $session_id"
195	    return 1
196	  }
197	
198	  print -r -- "$branch"
199	}
200	
201	_entire_resume_and_agent() {
202	  local target output session_id agent continue_command
203	  target="$1"
204	
205	  [[ -n "$target" ]] || return 1
206	
207	  output=$(command entire session resume "$target") || {
208	    print -r -- "$output"
209	    return 1
210	  }
211	
212	  print -r -- "$output"
213	
214	  continue_command=$(awk '/^[[:space:]]*(claude|codex)[[:space:]]+/ { sub(/^[[:space:]]+/, ""); print; exit }' <<< "$output")
215	
216	  case "$continue_command" in
217	    claude\ -r\ *)
218	      session_id=${continue_command##* }
219	      ;;
220	    codex\ resume\ *)
221	      session_id=${continue_command#codex resume }
222	      session_id=${session_id%% *}
223	      ;;
224	  esac
225	
226	  if [[ -z "$session_id" && "$target" == [0-9a-fA-F]##-[0-9a-fA-F-]## ]]; then
227	    session_id="$target"
228	  fi
229	
230	  [[ -n "$session_id" ]] || {
231	    print -u2 'Could not determine resumed session id.'
232	    return 1
233	  }
234	
235	  agent=$(command entire session info "$session_id" --json 2>/dev/null | jq -r '.agent // empty')
236	
237	  case "$agent" in
238	    'Claude Code')
239	      command claude -r "$session_id"
240	      ;;
241	    'Codex'|'OpenAI Codex')
242	      command codex resume "$session_id"
243	      ;;
244	    'GitHub Copilot'|'Copilot')
245	      command copilot --resume="$session_id"
246	      ;;
247	    *)
248	      print "Session restored for agent: ${agent:-unknown}"
249	      ;;
250	  esac
251	}
252	
253	_entire_session_action() {
254	  local line session_id checkpoint_id action branch
255	
256	  line=$(_entire_session_fzf 'down:70%:wrap') || return
257	  [[ -n "$line" ]] || return
258	
259	  session_id=$(cut -f1 <<< "$line")
260	  action=$(_entire_action_pick | awk -F ':' '{gsub(/^[[:space:]]+|[[:space:]]+$/, "", $1); print $1}') || return
261	
262	  case "$action" in
263	    resume)
264	      branch=$(_entire_branch_by_session "$session_id") || return
265	      _entire_resume_and_agent "$branch"
266	      ;;
267	    explain)
268	      checkpoint_id=$(_entire_latest_checkpoint_id_by_session "$session_id") || return
269	      command entire checkpoint explain "$checkpoint_id"
270	      ;;
271	    checkpoints)
272	      checkpoint_id=$(_entire_checkpoint_pick_by_session "$session_id") || return
273	      command entire checkpoint explain "$checkpoint_id"
274	      ;;
275	    info)
276	      command entire session info "$session_id"
277	      ;;
278	    stop)
279	      command entire session stop "$session_id"
280	      ;;
281	    clean)
282	      command entire clean --session "$session_id"
283	      ;;
284	    *)
285	      return 1
286	      ;;
287	  esac
288	}
289	
290	etf() {
291	  _entire_session_action
292	}
293	
294	etfd() {
295	  command entire dispatch --local
296	}
297	
298	etfr() {
299	  local branch
300	  branch=$(_entire_branch_pick) || return
301	  [[ -n "$branch" ]] || return
302	  _entire_resume_and_agent "$branch"
303	}
304

lastPrompt: repository 外だったら ghq をパイプさせたら便利なんじゃない？

aiTitle: Use ghq with pipe outside repository

content.content:
entire-fzf.plugin.zsh | 88 ++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++---
 1 file changed, 84 insertions(+), 4 deletions(-)

--- Changes ---

entire-fzf.plugin.zsh
  @@ -1,9 +1,9 @@
  -#   etf    # session picker -> action picker (explain/checkpoints/info/stop/clean)
  +#   etf    # session picker -> action picker (resume/explain/checkpoints/info/stop/clean)
   #   etfd   # entire dispatch --local
  -#   etfr   # pick branch and run entire session resume <branch>
  +#   etfr   # pick branch, resume session, then reopen the matching agent when supported
   
   _entire_session_list() {
     command entire session list --json 2>/dev/null |
  @@ -55,6 +55,7 @@ _entire_session_fzf() {
  +    'resume       : resume session and reopen matching agent' \
       'explain      : explain latest checkpoint' \
       'checkpoints  : pick checkpoint to explain' \
       'info         : session info' \
  @@ -174,8 +175,83 @@ _entire_checkpoint_pick_by_session() {
  +_entire_branch_by_session() {
  +  local session_id worktree_path branch
  +  session_id="$1"
  +
  +  [[ -n "$session_id" ]] || return 1
  +
  +  worktree_path=$(command entire session info "$session_id" --json 2>/dev/null | jq -r '.worktree_path // empty')
  +
  +  [[ -n "$worktree_path" ]] || {
  +    print -u2 "Could not find worktree_path for session: $session_id"
  +    return 1
  +  }
  +
  +  branch=$(git -C "$worktree_path" branch --show-current 2>/dev/null)
  +
  +  [[ -n "$branch" ]] || {
  +    print -u2 "Could not determine branch for session: $session_id"
  +    return 1
  +  }
  +
  +  print -r -- "$branch"
  +}
  +
  +_entire_resume_and_agent() {
  +  local target output session_id agent continue_command
  +  target="$1"
  +
  +  [[ -n "$target" ]] || return 1
  +
  +  output=$(command entire session resume "$target") || {
  +    print -r -- "$output"
  +    return 1
  +  }
  +
  +  print -r -- "$output"
  +
  +  continue_command=$(awk '/^[[:space:]]*(claude|codex)[[:space:]]+/ { sub(/^[[:space:]]+/, ""); print; exit }' <<< "$output")
  +
  +  case "$continue_command" in
  +    claude\ -r\ *)
  +      session_id=${continue_command##* }
  +      ;;
  +    codex\ resume\ *)
  +      session_id=${continue_command#codex resume }
  +      session_id=${session_id%% *}
  +      ;;
  +  esac
  +
  +  if [[ -z "$session_id" && "$target" == [0-9a-fA-F]##-[0-9a-fA-F-]## ]]; then
  +    session_id="$target"
  +  fi
  +
  +  [[ -n "$session_id" ]] || {
  +    print -u2 'Could not determine resumed session id.'
  +    return 1
  +  }
  +
  +  agent=$(command entire session info "$session_id" --json 2>/dev/null | jq -r '.agent // empty')
  +
  +  case "$agent" in
  +    'Claude Code')
  +      command claude -r "$session_id"
  +      ;;
  +    'Codex'|'OpenAI Codex')
  +      command codex resume "$session_id"
  +      ;;
  +    'GitHub Copilot'|'Copilot')
  +      command copilot --resume="$session_id"
  +      ;;
  +    *)
  +      print "Session restored for agent: ${agent:-unknown}"
  +      ;;
  +  esac
  +}
  +
   _entire_session_action() {
  -  local line session_id checkpoint_id action
  +  local line session_id checkpoint_id action branch
   
     line=$(_entire_session_fzf 'down:70%:wrap') || return
     [[ -n "$line" ]] || return
  @@ -184,6 +260,10 @@ _entire_session_action() {
  +    resume)
  +      branch=$(_entire_branch_by_session "$session_id") || return
  +      _entire_resume_and_agent "$branch"
  +      ;;
       explain)
         checkpoint_id=$(_entire_latest_checkpoint_id_by_session "$session_id") || return
         command entire checkpoint explain "$checkpoint_id"
  @@ -219,5 +299,5 @@ etfr() {
  -  command entire session resume "$branch"
  +  _entire_resume_and_agent "$branch"
   }
  +84 -4

     [[ -n "$line" ]] || return
  @@ -184,6 +260,10 @@ _entire_session_action() {
  +    resume)
  +      branch=$(_entire_branch_by_session "$session_id") || return
  +      _entire_resume_and_agent "$branch"
  +      ;;
       explain)
         checkpoint_id=$(_entire_latest_checkpoint_id_by_session "$session_id") || return
         command entire checkpoint explain "$checkpoint_id"
  @@ -219,5 +299,5 @@ etfr() {
  -  command entire session resume "$branch"
  +  _entire_resume_and_agent "$branch"
   }
  +84 -4

content.text:
はい、`_entire_resume_and_agent` / `etfr` あたりですね。今は `entire session resume` をそのまま実行しているだけなので、選んだセッションの `worktree_path` がカレントリポジトリの外（= ghq管理下の別リポジトリ）だと、たぶん意図通りに解決できません。

`ghq list -p` でローカルクローン一覧を取り、`worktree_path` のリモートURLやパスと突き合わせて該当リポジトリへ `cd`（または `entire` コマンドに `-C` 相当の指定）してから resume する、という流れにすれば「リポジトリ外のセッションを選んでもそのまま飛べる」体験になります。トレードオフは `ghq` への依存が増えること、かつ全リポジトリが ghq 管理下にある前提を置くことです。

この方向で実装してみましょうか、それとも別のフロー（`_entire_branch_pick` の候補を ghq 管理リポジトリ横断にする、など）を想定していましたか？

hookInfos.command: sh -c 'if ! command -v entire >/dev/null 2>&1; then exit 0; fi; exec entire hooks claude-code stop'

message.content: これ以上依存を増やすのはやめましょうか

content.text: 了解です、ghq への依存追加は見送りましょう。現状の `worktree_path` ベースの実装のままでいきます。他に手を入れたい箇所はありますか?

hookInfos.command: sh -c 'if ! command -v entire >/dev/null 2>&1; then exit 0; fi; exec entire hooks claude-code stop'

aiTitle: Use ghq with pipe outside repository
